title: How I'd Make Money with AI in 2026 (if I had to Start Over)

extracted.hook: I'll make over $1 million from AI this year and I'm only 23 years old.
extracted.credential_drop: "When I started, I was renting a crappy apartment and now I'm talking to you guys from my own place in Chicago after spending multiple days with 9 figure entrepreneurs like Alex Herozi and speaking at conferences all over the United States." (early)

accelerate: When I started, I was renting a crappy apartment and now I'm talking to you guys from my own place in Chicago after spending multiple days with 9 figure entrepreneurs like Alex Herozi and speaking at conferences all over the United States.
slow: The key is not to sell everything to everyone. It's to master one block at a time, prove you know how it works, and prove that it provides value to a client, and then only sell it to clients who need that specific outcome.

extracted.vulnerability: "I'll give you guys real steps you can take today to make your first few thousand all the way to your first million dollars with AI." (early)
extracted.cta: Go there to see the special pricing we've reserved for you. When you get on that page, you'll see everything we've talked about and you can get started immediately.
transitions: Now, here's a reality check and scores for this method.

Moving on to the practical application of this stage.

curiosity_gaps: "That's why the client was happy to pay, not because Jerome had a template, but for the outcome that the template delivered." (early)
"So here's your practical application. Don't try to master everything at once. Pick one template, learn it deeply, understand how to customize it, and sell that outcome." (early)
frameworks: Freelancer, start small, use templates as Lego blocks, and land your first $500 to $2,000 projects.
Consultant. Move up to strategy. Audit businesses, identify ROI, and design custom workflows.
Agency. Scale your consulting practice by hiring developers and sales support.
